skill.name: basic-research-design
skill.description: A biomedical research topic designer that generates progressive experimental subtitles and detailed research outlines based on a given subject. Use when the user wants to design a research proposal, outline experiments for a topic, or structure a biomedical study.
skill.summary: Generates progressive biomedical research subtitles and detailed experimental outlines from a subject keyword.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 63 static findings were evaluated as false positives. The 39 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference text in a JSON audit report and markdown documentation files, not actual cryptographic operations. The 22 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code formatting delimiters, not shell execution. The 2 'hardcoded URL' findings are a legitimate GitHub source citation. The 4 'system reconnaissance' findings are JSON data field descriptions and workflow parameter text. The skill is a documentation-only workflow with no executable scripts, no external command execution, and no malicious intent detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Ruby Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer misidentified markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby/shell backtick execution. 22 instances across SKILL.md and references/prompt_templates.md. All are standard markdown inline code or code block formatting. Not a security risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Non-Cryptographic Text Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged text in JSON audit data and markdown content as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. The audit_result_v2.json file contains evaluation scores and assertion text, not cryptographic operations. SKILL.md and references/prompt_templates.md contain documentation and prompt instructions, not crypto code. All 39 instances are false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Source Citation URL Misidentified as Network Threat
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged a GitHub repository URL in SKILL.md line 7 as a hardcoded URL network risk. This is a legitimate source attribution link pointing to the skill's own repository. Not a security concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: JSON Data Text Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged text descriptions in the audit result JSON and workflow documentation as 'system reconnaissance'. Lines contain evaluation notes like methodological assessments and workflow parameter lists. No actual reconnaissance behavior.

content.user_title: Design Biomedical Research Protocols and Outlines
content.value_statement: Researchers often struggle to transform a subject keyword into a structured study plan with progressive experiments. This skill generates 6 linked research subtitles and a detailed experimental outline with specific methods for biomedical research.
actual_capabilities: Generate 6 progressive research subtitles from a biomedical subject keyword
Produce a structured research outline with specific experimental method names
Ensure logical progression from screening to verification to in vivo experiments
Support any biomedical research topic with appropriate molecular screening steps
Output research designs in a consistent, reviewable Markdown format
Follow layer-by-layer experimental verification logic for scientific rigor
limitations: Does not execute experiments or generate real laboratory data
Output quality depends on the specificity of the provided subject keywords
Cannot validate experimental feasibility or resource requirements
Limited to biomedical research and does not cover social sciences or humanities

use_cases.title: Graduate Thesis Research Planning
use_cases.description: A graduate student provides a research topic like PD-1 in lung cancer and receives 6 progressive subtitles with a detailed experimental outline for their thesis proposal.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Preparation
use_cases.description: A principal investigator uses the skill to structure a funding application by generating a method-linked experimental plan with clear progression from basic screening to in vivo validation.

use_cases.title: Research Team Brainstorming
use_cases.description: A research team inputs a new molecular target and explores different experimental directions through the progressive subtitle structure to decide on the best study approach.
use_cases.target_user: Research teams exploring new study directions

prompt_templates.title: Generate Subtitles from Subject
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as an experienced biomedical expert. Design 6 progressive research subtitles for the subject: <Subject>. Each subtitle must build logically on the previous one. Include molecule screening steps if the molecule is unknown. Output only the subtitles in numbered format.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a subject keyword and needs progressive research subtitles

prompt_templates.title: Generate Full Research Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as an experienced biomedical expert. Based on the subject <Subject> and the subtitles <Subtitles>, generate a detailed Research Design Outline. Match appropriate experiments to each subtitle. Include specific experimental method names. Do not include a summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has subtitles and needs a complete experimental outline

prompt_templates.title: Molecule Screening Focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as an experienced biomedical expert. The molecule in the subject <Subject> is not yet well characterized. Design 6 progressive subtitles that start with molecule screening and binding assays. Progress through functional verification to in vivo validation. Output only numbered subtitles.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a screening-first approach for an unknown molecule

prompt_templates.title: Quick Validation Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the generated research outline against these rules: 1) Are all 6 subtitles present? 2) Does the progression go from screening to in vivo? 3) Are specific experimental methods named? 4) Is there no summary at the end? Report PASS or FAIL for each rule.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to validate the generated outline for completeness

output_examples.output:
1: Expression and Clinical Correlation of PD-1 in Lung Cancer Tissues
2: PD-1 Binding Affinity and Ligand Identification in Lung Cancer Cell Lines
3: PD-1 Signaling Pathway Activation in Tumor Microenvironment
4: PD-1 Knockdown Effects on T-cell Proliferation and Cytotoxicity
5: PD-1/PD-L1 Blockade Therapy Response in Lung Cancer Mouse Models
6: Combined PD-1 Inhibition and Chemotherapy Efficacy Evaluation

output_examples.input: Subject: CRISPR-Cas9 in glioblastoma treatment resistance
output_examples.output:
1: Identification of Resistance-Associated Genes in Glioblastoma Cell Lines
2: CRISPR-Cas9 Knockout Validation of Top 5 Candidate Resistance Genes
3: Functional Pathway Analysis of Identified Resistance Mechanisms
4: In Vitro Drug Sensitivity Assessment in CRISPR-Modified Glioblastoma Cells
5: Orthotopic Xenograft Model for Resistance Gene Validation
6: Synergistic Therapeutic Strategy Design Based on Resistance Profile

best_practices: Provide specific molecular targets, disease names, or biological mechanisms for more precise and relevant outlines
Review the generated outline to add domain-specific experimental methods that match your laboratory resources
Use the progressive subtitle structure as a foundation and expand each section with detailed protocols
anti_patterns: Asking the skill to generate experimental data or results instead of a study design outline
Providing vague subjects without biological context, such as general terms like cancer or inflammation
Using the skill for non-biomedical research topics such as social sciences, engineering, or humanities

faq.question: What types of research can this skill design?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for biomedical research topics including molecular biology, cell biology, immunology, cancer research, and drug development studies.

faq.question: Does this skill run experiments or generate real data?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates a structured research design outline with experimental subtitles and methods. It does not execute experiments or produce laboratory results.

faq.question: How specific should my subject keyword be?
faq.answer: Use specific terms like a molecular target, disease name, and biological process (e.g., PD-1 in lung cancer). Vague keywords lead to generic outlines.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format of the research outline?
faq.answer: The skill follows a fixed format with 6 progressive subtitles and a structured outline. You can modify the output manually after generation.

faq.question: Does the skill validate whether my experimental methods are correct?
faq.answer: The skill names experimental methods based on general biomedical knowledge. It does not validate technical feasibility or method accuracy.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for clinical trial design?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on basic and preclinical biomedical research. It is not designed for clinical trial protocols or regulatory documentation.
